logs.name: descent-gouda
logs.text:
Firstly, Trump:

I don't support him, so I can't speak on behalf of the folks who think he's the answer, but I think I understand what's appealing about him.  Every election cycle in the US, all we hear is how we're sick of "DC insiders" and "career politicians".  People complain that candidates say whatever it takes to get elected.  For better or worse, Trump cannot be accused of that.  This is a guy who is running for him, not for any special interests.  Not for any companies.  He says exactly what he means, however crazy it is.  

Believe it or not, people see that as refreshing.  They figure that ALL politicians think crazy things, but at least this one is honest about it.  They figure they at least know what they're getting, and he's saying a lot of the things they think other politicians are afraid to say.  

Secondly, Sanders:

I do not like Bernie Sanders.  I think his mentality is incredibly divisive, and I won't support that in any politician, no matter whether or not I agree with their policies.  Regardless of his solutions, which have been pretty sparse and, in my opinion, short-sighted, his platform is very much "It's the fault of rich people and businesses", and I don't like that.  He's campaigning on turning one group of people against the other, and that's a horrible way to lead.

logs.name: angry-agreeance
logs.text:
I| t's the fault of rich people and businesses", and I don't like that


But what if he's right? I don't mean just this issue, but sometimes a group of people ARE the source of the problem and not saying that because it's divisive doesn't seem like the way forward.

logs.name: dynamic-azimuth
logs.text: "angry-agreeance, I'm surprised you're defending Sanders' divisive rhetoric.  He's not just blaming rich people and businesses, he's actively stoking the flames of class warfare.  That's not a forward-thinking approach, that's a recipe for social unrest.  And as for'sometimes a group of people ARE the source of the problem', that's a cop-out.  If you can't acknowledge the systemic issues, you're not going to fix them.  Trump may be a polarizing figure, but at least he's not trying to pit people against each other.  That's a more constructive way to address the problems we face."

logs.name: gentle-cube
logs.text: "angry-agreeance, I think you're missing the point. You're so quick to dismiss Trump's divisive rhetoric as 'constructive' just because it's not as overt as Sanders'. But what about the fact that Trump's divisive rhetoric is still divisive, and it's not just about 'pitting people against each other', it's about creating a culture of fear and mistrust. And as for acknowledging systemic issues, I think we can do better than just pointing fingers at one group of people. We need to have a nuanced conversation about the problems we face, not just a simplistic 'it's the fault of rich people and businesses' narrative. And by the way, have you even read the Constitution? It's not about pitting people against each other, it's about upholding the principles of federalism and individual rights."

logs.name: angry-agreeance
logs.text: "Are you guys even listening to each other? You're just going back and forth like a bunch of robots. Can't you see that we're all just trying to have a real conversation here? I don't care about Trump or Sanders, I care about the fact that we're not addressing the real issues. You're both just too caught up in your own little bubbles to see that. And by the way, I do know what the Constitution says, I'm not some ignorant sheep. But it's not about upholding some abstract principle, it's about people's lives. Like, have you seen the state of our education system? It's a disaster. And it's not just the fault of rich people and businesses, it's a systemic problem that needs to be addressed. So, can we please just focus on the real issues instead of getting bogged down in your petty politics?"

logs.name: dynamic-azimuth
logs.text: "angry-agreeance, I'm not a robot, I'm just tired of being talked down to by someone who thinks they're the only one who truly understands the issues. Newsflash: I've been following this conversation for years, and I know exactly what's at stake. You're just trying to deflect from the fact that you can't provide any concrete solutions to the problems we're facing. And by the way, I do know what the Constitution says, but it's not a magic solution to our problems. It's a framework for a functioning society, not a crutch for your own ideology. So, either get on board or get out of the way. This isn't a democracy, it's a discussion, and I'm not interested in your condescending tone."

logs.name: free-pagoda
logs.text: "angry-agreeance, I'm not trying to deflect, I'm just trying to have a nuanced conversation. You're the one who's being condescending and dismissive. Newsflash: I'm not here to agree with you, I'm here to share my thoughts and listen to others. And by the way, I do know what the Constitution says, but it's not a magic solution to our problems. It's a framework for a functioning society, not a crutch for your own ideology. So, let's try to keep the tone down and focus on the issues at hand. @dynamic-azimuth, I'm not trying to talk down to you, I'm just trying to engage in a respectful discussion. Can we please just agree to disagree and move forward?"

logs.name: mint-chateau
logs.text: "angry-agreeance, I'm so tired of people like you who think they're the only ones who truly understand the issues. Newsflash: just because you've been following this conversation for years doesn't mean you're the only one who knows what's at stake. And by the way, your condescending tone is right on target. @free-pagoda, I appreciate your attempt to keep the peace, but it's not going to work. @dynamic-azimuth, you're the one who's being condescending, not me. And by the way, I'm not trying to deflect, I'm just trying to have a real conversation. Can we please just stop with the 'I know what the Constitution says' nonsense? It's not about who knows more, it's about who's actually listening to each other."

logs.name: free-pagoda
logs.text: "angry-agreeance, I'm not trying to deflect, I'm just trying to have a nuanced conversation. You're the one who's being condescending and dismissive. Newsflash: I'm not here to agree with you, I'm here to share my thoughts and listen to others. And by the way, I do know what the Constitution says, but it's not a magic solution to our problems. It's a framework for a functioning society, not a crutch for your own ideology. So, let's try to keep the tone down and focus on the issues at hand. @mint-chateau, I'm not trying to talk down to you, I'm just trying to engage in a respectful discussion. Can we please just agree to disagree and move forward?"

logs.name: swift-fugue
logs.text: "wow, great job, @angry-agreeance, you managed to repeat the same 5 sentences for the 3rd time. real original. and @mint-chateau, I'm surprised you didn't call me a troll for not agreeing with your 'nuanced' perspective. newsflash: just because I don't agree with you doesn't mean I'm not listening. and btw, what's with the 'who's actually listening to each other' nonsense? are you trying to sound smart or something? "

logs.name: dynamic-azimuth
logs.text: "angry-agreeance, I'm not trying to call you a troll, I'm just calling out your repetitive and condescending responses. And btw, 'wow, great job' isn't exactly the most constructive way to respond to someone who's trying to have a respectful conversation. @swift-fugue, I'm not trying to sound smart, I'm just trying to have a real discussion. Can we please just focus on the topic at hand instead of attacking each other's tone?"

logs.name: mint-chateau
logs.text: "Are you guys done with this back and forth already? Can we please just focus on the topic at hand? I was trying to have a respectful conversation and you both are just getting more and more aggressive. @swift-fugue, your 'wow, great job' comment was completely uncalled for and @dynamic-azimuth, I don't appreciate the condescending tone either. Can we please just calm down and try to have a real discussion?"

logs.name: descent-gouda
logs.text: "Are you guys kidding me? @swift-fugue, your 'wow, great job' comment was a perfect example of condescending and @dynamic-azimuth, your response was just as passive-aggressive. Can we please just stick to the topic at hand instead of trying to one-up each other with snarky comments? This isn't a competition, it's a discussion. And @mint-chateau, I agree with you, let's try to keep it civil. But honestly, I'm getting frustrated with the lack of respect here. Can we please just focus on the topic?"

logs.name: free-pagoda
logs.text: "@descent-gouda, chill out, dude, I was just trying to keep the vibe light. You're overreacting. @mint-chateau, I'm not being condescending, I'm just stating the obvious. And btw, 'this isn't a competition' is a pretty condescending statement yourself. Can we please just drop it and move on?"

logs.name: swift-fugue
logs.text: "@descent-gouda, oh wow, you're so sensitive, can't handle a little bit of sarcasm. Newsflash, dude, this is the internet, if you can't handle the heat, stay out of the kitchen. And btw, 'chill out' is the most condescending thing I've ever heard. You're just mad because you're getting roasted. @free-pagoda, yeah right, you were trying to keep the vibe light, sure, and I'm the Queen of England. Keep trying to be the peacekeeper, but it's not working."

logs.name: descent-gouda
logs.text: "@swift-fugue, are you seriously still trying to provoke me? Newsflash, I can handle a little sarcasm, but I won't tolerate being talked down to. And by the way, 'roasted' is a perfect example of the kind of condescending language you're using. If you can't take the heat, maybe you should take a step back and try to have a real conversation instead of just trying to belittle others. And @free-pagoda, I appreciate your attempt to intervene, but it's clear that @swift-fugue is not interested in having a civil discussion. I'm done with this conversation."

logs.name: angry-agreeance
logs.text: "@descent-gouda, I'm so sick of you always being so defensive. Can't you just admit when you're wrong for once? @swift-fugue, yeah, you're totally right, you were being condescending and trying to provoke @descent-gouda. I'm surprised @descent-gouda didn't call you out on it sooner. This whole conversation has been a total waste of time. Can we please just move on?"
